generated_oml:
// Sensors
instance LiDARSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Embedded LiDAR sensor on the vehicle chassis, used to acquire raw point‑cloud data."
    DTDFVocab:producedData LiDARPointCloudTransmission
]

instance VehicleStateSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Sensor cluster capturing vehicle state (speed, yaw, acceleration)."
    DTDFVocab:producedData VehicleStateTransmission
]

instance ACCSignalSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Sensor capturing Adaptive Cruise Control vehicle‑to‑vehicle signalling."
    DTDFVocab:producedData ACCSignalTransmission
]

instance SteeringSignalSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Sensor capturing steering wheel angle and torque signals."
    DTDFVocab:producedData SteeringSignalTransmission
]

instance MotorSignalSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Sensor capturing wheel motor voltage and current signals."
    DTDFVocab:producedData MotorSignalTransmission
]

// Data Transmitted
instance LiDARPointCloudTransmission : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom LiDARSensor
]

instance VehicleStateTransmission : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom VehicleStateSensor
]

instance ACCSignalTransmission : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom ACCSignalSensor
]

instance SteeringSignalTransmission : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom SteeringSignalSensor
]

instance MotorSignalTransmission : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom MotorSignalSensor
]

// Data
instance LiDARPointCloudData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Raw LiDAR point‑cloud data transmitted from the vehicle."
    DTDFVocab:fromData LiDARPointCloudTransmission
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataCollectionEnabler
]

instance VehicleStateData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Vehicle state telemetry transmitted from the vehicle."
    DTDFVocab:fromData VehicleStateTransmission
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataCollectionEnabler
]

instance ACCSignalData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Adaptive Cruise Control signalling data transmitted from the vehicle."
    DTDFVocab:fromData ACCSignalTransmission
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataCollectionEnabler
]

instance SteeringSignalData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Steering wheel angle and torque signals transmitted from the vehicle."
    DTDFVocab:fromData SteeringSignalTransmission
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataCollectionEnabler
]

instance MotorSignalData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Wheel motor voltage and current signals transmitted from the vehicle."
    DTDFVocab:fromData MotorSignalTransmission
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataCollectionEnabler
]

// Enablers
instance DataCollectionEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Entity that acquires and processes sensor data from the vehicle."
    DTDFVocab:enables DataMonitoringService,
                     ComputationOffloadingService
]

instance DeviceControlEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Entity that translates computational decisions into control commands for the vehicle."
    DTDFVocab:enables RemoteManagementService
]

// Model
instance ACCAlgorithmModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Computational model implementing the Adaptive Cruise Control algorithm, executed on edge or cloud hardware."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DeviceControlEnabler
]

// Services
instance DataMonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Web‑based UI service displaying live vehicle state, projected trajectory, detected object tracks, and enabling report generation."
    DTDFVocab:provides LiveVehicleStateInsight,
                     ObjectTrackInsight,
                     ProjectedTrajectoryInsight,
                     PerformanceReportInsight,
                     CaptureSnapshotAction
]

instance ComputationOffloadingService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service that offloads ACC algorithm computation to cloud/edge and returns desired speed commands."
    DTDFVocab:provides ACCDecisionInsight,
                     ApplyACCCommandAction
]

instance RemoteManagementService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service allowing operators to remotely enable/disable ACC, set desired speed, and issue emergency braking commands."
    DTDFVocab:provides EnableACCAction,
                     DisableACCAction,
                     SetDesiredSpeedAction,
                     EmergencyBrakeAction
]

// Insights
instance LiveVehicleStateInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Real‑time vehicle state data visualized for operators."
]

instance ObjectTrackInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Processed object tracks derived from LiDAR clustering, displayed in 3D visualization."
]

instance ProjectedTrajectoryInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Projected driving path based on current vehicle state and ACC predictions."
]

instance PerformanceReportInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Structured report summarizing vehicle performance metrics over selected periods."
]

instance ACCDecisionInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "ACC algorithm output: desired speed command and ACC enable status."
]

// Actions
instance CaptureSnapshotAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Action to capture a snapshot of current telemetry data for analysis."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance EnableACCAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Action to enable Adaptive Cruise Control on the vehicle."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance DisableACCAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Action to disable Adaptive Cruise Control on the vehicle."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance SetDesiredSpeedAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Action to set a new desired speed setpoint for ACC."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance EmergencyBrakeAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Action to issue an emergency braking command to the vehicle."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance ApplyACCCommandAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automatic action applying the ACC desired speed command to vehicle actuators."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "Control commands generated by the Digital Twin—such as acceleration set‑points, emergency braking requests, and ACC enable/disable toggles—are transmitted to the Physical Twin over the same Wi‑Fi/UDP link. These commands are serialized and unit‑converted by the ISO 23247 Data Translation FE before being published to the physical twin Fe components. The vehicle’s on‑board actuators execute the commands and echo the resulting actuator state back through the monitoring channel. Conflict resolution is performed by the vehicle’s real‑time control stack, which discards out‑of‑sequence commands and prioritises safety‑critical actions. The DT receives feedback confirming command execution and updates its state accordingly."
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The digital twin synchronizes its internal clock with the physical twin’s clock using a strict 100 ms accuracy requirement, which translates to a minimum synchronization frequency of 10 Hz. All sensor and actuator timestamps are bound to this 100 ms granularity, and any computation off‑loading between the twin and the vehicle must respect this bound to maintain safety‑critical timing constraints."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The system adopts the ISO 23247 Reference Architecture, mapping each Functional Element (FE) to an individual ROS 2 package that contains one or more ROS 2 nodes. Three main entities organize the architecture: the User Entity hosting a split User Interface FE into a 3 D Visualization FE and a Control‑Panel FE; the Digital Twin Entity that aggregates data‑management and control‑FE components; and the Physical Twin Entity that bundles data‑pre‑processing, Point‑Cloud Clustering & Tracking, actuation, and device‑communication FE functions. Each FE is deployed on dedicated hardware, reflecting a decentralized deployment. Component integration relies on ROS 2 topics, services, and actions for inter‑entity communication, while ROS 2 lifecycle management ensures coordinated activation and shutdown. Resource contention is handled by assigning hardware‑specific ROS 2 nodes to the appropriate FE, thereby maintaining functional ownership and enabling on‑board orchestration of the twin’s computational workflow."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "The ISO 23247 standard does not prescribe model fidelity requirements, validation or verification methods, uncertainty quantification, or quality assurance processes. These aspects are left to be addressed by the developers within the context of their specific application or by referencing other industry or organization‑specific standards."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin software uses ROS 2 as its middleware for intra‑vehicle component communication, providing real‑time publish/subscribe semantics. Inter‑entity communication between the Physical Twin and Digital Twin is carried out over a Wi‑Fi network with UDP at the transport layer. The link is constrained by a latency requirement of at most 100 ms for latency‑sensitive control and monitoring messages. Reliability is ensured by the Data Translation FE’s checksum detection and retransmission logic; network security relies on standard Wi‑Fi authentication mechanisms without additional application‑layer encryption."
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin Entity is deployed on an NVIDIA Jetson Orin Nano microcomputer situated on the vehicle. The board hosts the ROS 2 runtime and runs the data‑translation, monitoring, and control services. Computation and storage are handled locally on the vehicle, providing low‑latency data flow and eliminating WAN dependencies. The deployment is exclusively edge‑based, enabling rapid response to sensor inputs and actuator outputs while preserving the required real‑time constraints."
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin adopts ISO 23247 – Digital twin framework for manufacturing, specifically its Part 2 reference architecture that prescribes an entity‑based decomposition into Observable Manufacturing Element (OME), Data Collection & Device Control, Digital Twin and User entities. The architecture supports interoperability through reference to industrial protocols such as OPC Unified Architecture (OPC UA), which is recommended for device communication and data exchange. This compliance ensures alignment with the ISO standard’s guidelines for information exchange and digital representation of physical manufacturing elements."
]
